nameMeanings.name: Karlsson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Lars, Anders, Alf, Bjorn, Erik, Kerstin, Agust, Elnar, Fritjof, Gudmundur, Johan, Kristinn.
Swedish: patronymic from the personal name   Karl   (see also   Charles  ). Compare   Carlsson  .

variants.name: Karlson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Algot, Lars, Nils, Eskil, Holger, Knute, Lennart, Sven.  German Kurt, Hans, Otto.
Americanized form (and a very rare Swedish variant) of Swedish   Karlsson  .
Americanized form of Norwegian and Danish   Karlsen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Karl
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Lothar, Erwin, Erhard, Georg, Otto, Bernd, Bernhard, Ernst, Gerhard, Hartmut, Heinz.

German: from the German personal name Karl, from Old High German karl ‘man, husband, freeman’. The popularity of this name and its cognates in central and northern Europe was greatly enhanced by its status as a royal and imperial name; in particular it was bestowed in honor of the Frankish emperor Charlemagne (about 742–814; Latin name Carolus Magnus). This surname is also found in some other European countries, e.g. in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, and Croatia, but is relatively rare there. See also Carl and Charles .

nameMeanings.name: Birger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse: apparently an agent derivative of biarga ‘to help’. Earlier forms of the name are Birghir and Byrghir. It has been in use from the Viking period to the present day, and it may in some cases have been chosen as a masculine form of Birgit .
